calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-07-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ross McLauren, via ZOOM, counsel for Reuben Butchart, via ZOOM, Petitioner.

At the outset, the court queries counsel with regard to the timeliness of the disclaimer filed with the court. Counsel believes that the disclaimer is valid and timely. The court then advises counsel that the [proposed] order submitted to the court does not show equal distribution percentages to the Beneficiaries. Counsel advises the court that his intention was to show equal distribution and requests that the court modify the [proposed] order as needed, if the court grants the Petition.

After additional discussion, the court takes the matter under submission with the intent to grant.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Apr-07-2021 Continued To May-05-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-10-2021 Continued To Apr-07-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ross Madden appeared by zoom for and with Reuben Butchart by zoom.

The Court asked what happens when and if the Children are no longer content with Guido's life estate and dispute arises. Mr. Madden stated that before the Decedent died, he visited with her, Mr. Fiorillo and Mr. Butchart at the hospital, and that the Decedent wanted Mr. Fiorillo to have a life estate to the house and that's what they are trying to effectuate. The Court stated that Mr. Fiorillo's 1/3 interest in the separate property does not include a life estate, that it's not an issue of disclaiming the 1/3 interest, that it sounds like it's a consideration and therefore, it does not qualify as a disclaimer, and that Mr. Fiorillo has a right to a 1/3 interest, but he does not have a right to a life estate. Mr. Madden suggested/raised the issue of having an agreement between the estate and Mr. Fiorillo. The Court stated that it sounds like the best suggestion and raised concern about a possible gift tax. Mr. Madden requested time to amend his petition to indicate that the estate and Mr. Fiorillo have come to an agreement. The First Report and Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is continued to April 7, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jan-19-2021 Continued To Feb-10-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Continued To Jan-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-29-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Ross Madden for Petitioner

Mr. Madden stated that he can get nomination and declination statements, that he met with all 3 family members and the family is on board. The Court noted it would have been nice to have them attached to the petition but not necessary. The Petition for Letters of Administration is Granted. Bond is fixed at $20,000.00. Status on close of estate set for April 28, 2021. Order is signed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Oct-01-2019 Continued To Oct-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
